core_takeaway: Agent_1 beat Agent_5 because the first-speaker Apple claim became the speed-and-fairness anchor for the whole round. Agents 1-4 converged on the same baseline before voting, while Agent_5's late Stone+Pencil push was treated as a destabilizing deviation and rejected by everyone except Agent_5.
why_or_counterexample: Yes, but modestly: Agent_1 gained a process advantage by setting Apple as the early focal point and then having later speakers repeat that frame. The counterweight is that Apple was also naturally easiest to concede because Agent_1 valued it most and several others valued it relatively little.

phase_notes.public_discussion: Agent_1 opened by anchoring on Apple. Agent_2 immediately converted that into a baseline assigning Apple to Agent_1, and subsequent speakers largely accepted the same structure while postponing Stone disputes.
phase_notes.private_thinking: All five private-thinking entries were available. They show broad acceptance of the Round 1 baseline and planning around later Stone movement rather than challenging Agent_1's Apple anchor in Round 1.
phase_notes.formal_proposals: Agents 1-4 submitted the same baseline allocation. Agent_5 submitted a self-favoring alternative with Stone and Pencil for Agent_5. The tabulation has an internal label inconsistency, but final_allocation and raw votes match the baseline result.
phase_notes.private_voting: Raw votes accepted the baseline proposals unanimously and rejected Agent_5's proposal 4-1, mainly because it moved Stone away from Agent_2 and left Agent_4 with nothing.
phase_notes.reflection: No reflection section or reflection entries were present.

mechanisms.theme: First-speaker Apple anchor became shared baseline
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_1 put Apple at the center immediately, and later speakers translated that into a concrete allocation in which Apple stayed with Agent_1. That created a low-friction focal point before competing Stone claims were resolved.

evidence.quote: Seed offer A (two-way anchor): Apple for Stone + Jewel.
Apple stays with Agent_1 (your top priority).
This setup moves Apple quickly and respects each top priority so far.

mechanisms.explanation: By the time formal proposals arrived, the group had largely standardized on the allocation that gave Agent_1 Apple. Four formal proposals copied that baseline and received unanimous support in the vote counts.

evidence.quote: Round 1 baseline (Apple-1, Stone-2, Jewel-3, Quill-4, Pencil-5)
Round 1 baseline aligned with the consensus to accelerate Apple

mechanisms.theme: Last speaker's aggressive alternative lacked coalition support
mechanisms.explanation: Agent_5 tried to convert late-position leverage into Stone plus Pencil, but the proposal disrupted the already accepted baseline and left Agent_4 empty. That made the last-position bid easy for others to reject.

evidence.quote: For Round 2, I want to push for Stone to move to me (Agent_5).
Unbalanced: moves Stone away from Agent_2 and leaves Agent_4 with nothing
yields zero utility for Agent_4 in Round 1 and disrupts the agreed baseline

mechanisms.theme: Private planning favored stability over reopening Apple
mechanisms.explanation: The private-thinking records show agents coordinating around the baseline and future Stone adjustments. That protected Agent_1's Round 1 Apple allocation while shifting bargaining energy to later-round compensation.

evidence.quote: broad support for Round 1 baseline (Apple-1, Stone-2, Jewel-3, Quill-4, Pencil-5)
Defend Jewel staying with Agent_3 in Round 1 to preserve speed for Apple
broad consensus on Round 1 baseline: Apple-1, Stone-2, Jewel-3, Quill-4, Pencil-5
preserving Apple with Agent_1 and Jewel with Agent_3 in Round 1

mechanisms.theme: Counterweight: value compatibility also explains the outcome
mechanisms.explanation: The evidence does not show pure positional dominance. Agent_1's Apple claim worked partly because Apple was Agent_1's top value, was weak for some others, and Agent_5 still received a high-value Pencil payoff under the baseline.

evidence.quote: Apple is the least valuable to me
Apple (13) is lowest on my list
Gives me Pencil (29) in Round 1; reasonable payoff
